Title: Police strengthen convoy SOPs following fatal FRU crash, says IGP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: One year after the fatal Federal Reserve Unit (FRU) crash that sparked nationwide scrutiny over police operational safety and welfare standards, police convoy standards have been improved with upgraded logistics and strengthened protection measures for personnel.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the improvements followed continuous internal reviews, particularly involving large-scale convoy movements and deployments.
"We have streamlined our SOPs in carrying out assignments, especially those involving large convoys.
"With the improvements in SOPs and regulations that have been established, we can reduce accidents not only during operations but also while personnel are on the road," he told reporters after closing a two-day special police dialogue session at the Kuala Lumpur Golf and Country Club today,
The tragedy on May 13 last year reignited debate over operational safety standards, transport conditions and welfare protection involving frontline police personnel.
The incident occurred along Jalan Chikus-Sungai Lampam in Teluk Intan, Perak, about 8.50am when a
truck carrying 18 members
of FRU Unit 5 from the Sungai Senam Camp in Ipoh was hit by a gravel-laden lorry that veered into the path of the FRU vehicle.
The FRU personnel were travelling in a convoy back to Ipoh after completing duties in Teluk Intan.
Khalid said the department was also continuously upgrading operational facilities and transportation assets to improve personnel safety and efficiency.
"Those matters are continuously being improved from time to time.
"At the same time, we have received allocations from the government to enhance equipment and other logistics, including new vehicles, which I believe will further boost the morale of the police," he said.
On welfare assistance for families affected by the tragedy, Khalid said the police had implemented the necessary support, including insurance-related assistance.
"Up to now, we have implemented all required assistance, insurance and related matters properly and accordingly," he said.
Khalid said safety remained a priority within the force, adding that personnel were constantly reminded to comply with established SOPs while carrying out their duties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Evolving drone war in southern Lebanon clouds Iran peace prospects
Article:
BEIRUT: While Washington and Tehran argue over a deal to end the attacks on shipping that are shaking the world economy, Iran's most powerful ally Hizbollah and Israel are stepping up a drone war in Lebanon - on camera - that is complicating the path to peace.
In recent weeks, Hizbollah has used cheap, easy-to-assemble First Person View kamikaze drones to transform the war it has been fighting since it began firing on Israel on March 2, days after the US-Israeli forces began their attacks on Iran.
Controlled with fiber-optic cables, the FPV drones can evade Israel's high-tech jamming technologies to target its troops occupying southern Lebanon during a shaky ceasefire announced on April 16, a week after the truce in the wider Iran war began.
The Iran-backed group has published videos of more than 45 FPV attacks, 28 of them in the nearly four weeks since the ceasefire, which had halted Israeli attacks on the Lebanese capital before Israel said it targeted a Hizbollah commander there on Thursday.
The truce has also left Israeli ground forces occupying a so-called buffer zone up to 10 km (six miles) in from the border, in confined territory, which Hizbollah knows well, and vulnerable to such attacks.
All of the videos before the ceasefire was announced showed UAVs flying at static positions or vehicles including tanks and excavators, with no fatalities reported by Israel.
But since the ceasefire was announced, Hizbollah began targeting groups of soldiers, reporting five attacks.
Three Israeli soldiers and one contractor were reported by Israel to have been killed.
Israel is firing back, with at least two deadly FPV drone attacks against Hizbollah in April complete with published drone images purporting to show Hizbollah fighters up close.
The widespread use of FPV attack drones began several years ago and thousands of kilometres away in Ukraine, where front lines are covered with netting to defend against Russia's drones, and where some drone operators are watching Hizbollah.
"They are amateurs, but they are learning," said Dmytro Putiata, a drone warfare expert serving in Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Brigades.
Iran and mediator Pakistan say any US-Iranian peace agreement must include a halt to Israeli strikes in Lebanon to prevent an escalation there restarting the wider Iran war.
US-mediated direct talks between the Lebanese government and Israel are due to resume on Thursday and Friday, but progress has been slow; Israel insists that Lebanon disarm Hizbollah, which risks reigniting conflict in a country that suffered a 1975-1990 civil war.
Hizbollah's head of media relations, Youssef el-Zein, said the group assessed that continued Israeli troop casualties from FPV drones could force an Israeli withdrawal more effectively than the negotiations with Israel, which Hizbollah opposes.
Israeli troops who have invaded southern Lebanon in the current conflict presented "an opportunity, and not a threat," as they could be more easily targeted, he said.
"We know the enemy's supremacy, but we also know their points of weakness. We are taking advantage of the points of weakness to create that balance," Zein told reporters.
[[nid:1438785]]
According to a Hizbollah commander, a specialised drone unit works with the organisation's procurement team to purchase parts from various markets.
They are checked for signs of Israeli interference, according to a Lebanese military source briefed on Hizbollah's drone usage.
The group has been on high alert since thousands of its communication devices were booby-trapped and detonated by Israel in 2024.
Hizbollah's first FPV video shows an attack dated March 22, three weeks into the war. The first footage showing its drone components, including the warhead, is dated April 11.
"The drones shown in the imagery all show systems assembled from parts commonly made by Chinese enterprises and sold freely on the online marketplaces," said Konrad Iturbe, a drone expert based in Spain with experience flying and modifying commercial quadcopters.
A basic drone costs less than US$400, according to the Hizbollah commander and an Israeli drone expert.
Reuters geolocated the attacks to towns running the entire strip of Lebanon's border area, showing the breadth of their deployment.
A Russian PG-7L highly explosive anti-tank warhead was fitted on the drone in the April 11 footage, according to a drone operator in Ukraine who declined to be named for security reasons and a foreign security official tracking Hizbollah's drones.
Hizbollah's arsenal already included those warheads, the foreign official said, but fitting them onto a drone made them a longer-range, precision weapon.
Asked whether Hezbollah was relying on Russian drone expertise, Zein said the group had in-house experts.
Founded in 1982 with help from Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, Hizbollah, which has tens of thousands of rockets and precision missiles, began developing drone capabilities in 2004 and used them in 2006 and 2024 wars.
The drone operator in Ukraine said the Hizbollah pilots appeared to have had a few weeks of training. He said the spool in the April 11 footage was consistent with a canister holding about 10 km (six miles) of fiber-optic wiring to link drone and pilot - a link that the Hizbollah commander said was key.
"The objective is that Israeli radar systems cannot detect them, effectively blinding the enemy," he said.
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has acknowledged the drones are a problem. "A few weeks ago, I ordered the establishment of a special project to thwart the drone threat... It will take time, but we are on it," he said on May 3.
The Israeli military has reported near-daily explosive drones launched at its forces in southern Lebanon. Israel's Army Radio says they have hurt as many as 40 troops.
An Israeli defence official said that the drones were harder to detect and neutralise because they are small, and are flown "low and slow" by Hizbollah crews who know the topography well.
[[nid:1438784]]
ALMA, an Israeli think tank, said Hizbollah's attacks during the ceasefire predominantly used drones and the dissemination of footage created "significant psychological impact."
Israeli critics say solutions should have already been found. The defence official, who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss sensitive matters, said there was no quick fix.
Israel's defence establishment has been looking at Ukraine and studying the drone threat for over a year, he said. New defence measures could be deployed within weeks to months.
While high-tech solutions are being developed, low-tech solutions, like nets, will be deployed and enhancements to soldiers' rifles were expected to help take down the drones too, the defence official said.
The Israeli military has also been using its Iron Dome missile interceptor system and has boosted radar detection, a senior Israeli military official said.
A newly developed drone interception system was tested by the Air Force in April, the official said, but it failed.
Both the officials said that the best defence is striking the Hizbollah crews operating the drones.
Israel published a video on April 13 of a target covering his face as a drone approaches and another on April 29 targeting a fighter on a motorbike. Israel has not published images of its own drones.
Iturbe said some Hizbollah pilots seemed to have moved from easier but less effective fixed-angle flying to pitching down, speeding up and hitting vehicles from above.
"Lesson clearly learned here," he said.
Still, Hizbollah's videos show drones mostly targeting armoured vehicles, not soldiers, with few consecutive attacks on a target, or shots from a second drone or surveillance position.
"Individual clips of vehicles being struck are great for political videos, but do not necessarily translate into military effect," forensic imagery analyst William Goodhind said.
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: M'sian taxi drivers, tricked into smuggling contraband into Singapore
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A 71-year-old Malaysian taxi driver was detained during a routine checkpoint inspection in Singapore's Woodlands Checkpoint on April 3.
The republic's Immigration & Checkpoints Authority (ICA) officers acting on profiling checks, subjected the vehicle to enhanced inspection.
The taxi driver's 28-year-old Singaporean passenger had more than 40 vape devices and components, and 26 packets of unpaid duty cigarettes, in his backpack.
The elderly driver and another passenger, 73, were also detained. They were, however, later released after it was established that they were not involved in the smuggling attempt.
Under Singapore law, the importation, possession and sale of vaping products is strictly prohibited, while offences involving duties-unpaid cigarettes carry penalties which include heavy fines and imprisonment.
For vape-related offences, fines up to SG$10,000 (RM31,067) and six months imprisonment term may apply, while for contraband cigarettes, offenders can be fined 40 times the amount and jailed up to six years.
A Johor Baru-based taxi driver, Siew Toh, 56, who frequently ferries passengers across the Causeway, said drivers are increasingly exposed to risk but have limited legal ability to intervene.
"We don't have any law that allows us to check passengers' bags. We just drive," Siew said, adding he would always caution passengers not to carry anything illegal and remind them to dispose of such items before the journey begins.
"However, sometimes we only realise the problem when we get caught at the checkpoint. By then, it's already too late."
He said drivers are often left stranded for days when passengers are detained, as the drivers are wanted by the Singapore authorities to facilitate investigations to ensure that they are not in cohorts with the "smugglers."
"Most of the time, we are detained and later released, but we can be held for up to a week which affects our income. We still have to pay rental for the vehicle and other cost of living expenses even while we are stuck," he said.
He added that drivers also risk being flagged during future entries, even if they are not involved in any wrongdoing.
"It's a hassle. Once your vehicle is linked to a case, you worry every time you cross the causeway," he said.
The New Straits Times learnt that there are more than a worrying number of cases in Singapore, involving Malaysian taxi drivers, who were arrested after they had allegedly ferried passengers concealing contraband goods.
In Singapore ignorance of contents is not a defence if individuals are found to have accepted delivery tasks without proper verification.
Johor Baru-Singapore Hired Vehicle Owners, Drivers and Taxi-Cab Association president Mohd Suhaimi Saidi said drivers are at risk, especially when their passengers are dishonest.
Suhaimi said there are three Johor Baru-Singapore hired-drivers associations under their wing which is known as G3, with 200 registered drivers and vehicle owners.
"Although passengers are always reminded to declare any contraband, no passengers do it. Takde barang larangan (no prohibited goods) is the typical answers they get.
"The drivers do not have the authority under the law to check their bags. We always tell our drivers to inform their passengers that there is no duty-free allowance for tobacco products in Singapore and bringing in vape products is illegal.
"At the end of the day, it's the drivers who are taking the risk. If they are caught in Singapore, they are subjected to a urine test and very likely to be held for at least three days before they are released, if investigations showed that they are not involved in the smuggling attempt," Suhaimi said.
Suhaimi said it was high time for the Transport Ministry to come out with a solution.
"Ideally the ministry should get the Road Transport Department to install x-ray baggage scanners at the Larkin taxi terminal or station their officers at the terminal to check the luggage to ensure drivers are not put at risk," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police say Jho Low probe ongoing despite pardon reports, says IGP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police investigations into fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, also known as Jho Low, are continuing despite reports that he sought a pardon from United States President Donald Trump.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail confirmed this today when asked to comment on Low's latest move to seek leniency from prosecution for his alleged role in the multibillion-ringgit 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) scandal.
"This Jho Low issue is still an ongoing investigation. I see matters like this as highly sensitive for us to discuss openly," he said after closing a two-day special dialogue at the Kuala Lumpur Golf and Country Club today.
The Wall Street Journal recently reported that Low had allegedly sought assistance linked to Trump in an attempt to obtain relief from legal action in the United States.
Low remains wanted by authorities in several countries over his alleged role in the 1MDB scandal.
Khalid said Malaysia continued to maintain cooperation with relevant foreign enforcement authorities in relation to ongoing investigations.
"I believe everything remains in good condition and there are no issues for us in carrying out our duties.
"We maintain good relations with the relevant countries.
"Insya-Allah, we will continue carrying out our responsibilities," he said.
Authorities have repeatedly maintained that efforts to trace and repatriate Low remain ongoing through international cooperation and intelligence-sharing with foreign agencies.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 70 children killed in West Bank, East Jerusalem since 2025: Unicef
Article:
GENEVA: Seventy children have been killed in the occupied Palestinian territories excluding Gaza since early 2025, amounting to around one a week, while over 800 have been injured, the UN children's agency said on Tuesday.
Most of those killed or injured in the West Bank and East Jerusalem were hit by live ammunition but others were stabbed, beaten or pepper-sprayed, Unicef said.
"These are not isolated incidents. They point to a sustained pattern of the worst kind of violation - violations against children," spokesperson James Elder told a Geneva press briefing after visiting the West Bank last week.
He said 93 per cent of the children who had lost their lives since January 2025 had been killed by Israeli forces.
The others were victims of settler attacks, unexploded ordnance or accidental hits by Palestinian forces, he said.
Human rights groups have described a renewed surge in violence against Palestinians by Israeli settlers and soldiers since 2023.
The United Nations and most countries deem Israel's settlements on West Bank land captured ​in the 1967 war illegal.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 38 accident cases linked to drink-driving reported in Penang since 2021
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang recorded a total of 38 road accidents involving drivers under the influence of alcohol between 2021 and April this year.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state recorded six cases in 2021, which declined to five cases in 2022, before rising again to 13 cases in 2023 and 14 cases as of April this year.
Chow said various enforcement measures have been implemented to curb drunk-driving incidents, including regular operations against driving under the influence at high-risk accident zones during nighttime hours and at entertainment hotspots.
"Integrated operations are also conducted with the Road Transport Department (RTD) and local authorities to strengthen enforcement on the ground," he said in a written reply to Mohd Sobri Saleh (Pas-Permatang Berangan).
Sobri had sought details on drunk-driving accident statistics as well as enforcement and preventive measures to raise public awareness of the dangers of driving under the influence of alcohol.
Chow, who is also state finance, economic development, land and communications committee chairman, said prevention efforts also included Op Didik and advocacy programmes at public locations such as shopping malls, factories and schools.
He said awareness campaigns have been stepped up through social media platforms, including Facebook and TikTok, as well as radio and television broadcasts to educate the public on the risks of drunk driving.
"The state will continue to adopt a dual approach of enforcement and education to curb the rising cases and improve road safety," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel strikes kill 8 people in south Beirut, including 2 children
Article:
BEIRUT: Israeli strikes targeting cars on a highway south of Beirut on Wednesday killed eight people, including two children, Lebanon's health ministry said, in the latest attacks despite a ceasefire in the Israel-Hizbollah war.
In a statement, the ministry said "the three raids carried out by the Israeli enemy on the Saida-South coastal highway – specifically in Barja, Jiyeh and Saadiyat – resulted in 8 martyrs, including two children."
Earlier, the Israeli military said it launched a new wave of strikes across southern Lebanon Wednesday, targeting Hizbollah infrastructure, despite a truce with the Lebanese government to halt fighting with the armed group.
"The IDF has begun striking Hizbollah terror infrastructure in several areas in southern Lebanon," the military said, hours after issuing fresh evacuation warnings for a number of villages in the area.
On Tuesday, Israeli strikes killed 13 people in southern Lebanon including a soldier, a child and two rescue workers, the health ministry said.
"A strike on the city of Nabatieh left five dead, including two Civil Defence rescuers," the ministry said, while another strike in around Jebchit left four dead "including a soldier and a Syrian national" and a third strike in Bint Jbeil killed "four civilians, including a child and a woman".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Victims claim up to RM133k lost in alleged bogus aesthetic training scam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Victims claim they lost up to RM133,000 after enrolling in an alleged fake aesthetic training programme involving forged certifications, improper medical procedures and false credentials.
At a press conference at Wisma MCA, today, MCA Public Services and Complaints Department head Datuk Seri Michael Chong described the course, allegedly conducted by a "professor", as "fake and illegal."
He said the programme misused academic titles and institutions, including purported links to Universiti Malaya qualifications and master's degrees.
"Even the dean herself has verified that it's all fake. Her name was used," he said, adding that a police report had been lodged over the alleged misuse of the dean's identity and signature.
He said the mastermind behind the programme, described as a "professor" and principal of two associated colleges, was also believed to have used falsified certificates and credentials.
Four victims came forward, with two sharing their experiences during the briefing.
The first victim, Wong, in her 30s, said she paid about RM133,000 after being persuaded through Facebook advertisements.
"I paid around RM133,000. Then I was charged additional fees for licences, insurance and clinic opening expenses," she said.
She said she never received proper certification despite making payments for nearly two years.
Another victim, Lee, also in her 30s, said she lost about RM109,000 after enrolling in what she believed was a legitimate programme conducted by local and foreign "professors" and "doctors."
Lee said she received a qualification certificate and licence upon completing the course and was given a Universiti Malaya student card.
"Everything looked legitimate. There were even 'professors' and 'doctors' guiding us throughout the course," she said.
She alleged that timing and location for practical and theory classes were disclosed only periodically.
"Sometimes, participants were made to practise on real patients at hotels arranged by the organisers," she said.
Legal representative Esther Ho said checks with the Higher Education Ministry found AIS International College was not a registered institution and not authorised to issue academic certificates.
She said victims who attended courses under the Association of International Certified Aesthetician (AICA) and AIS later discovered their names were not listed in Universiti Malaya's system.
"They checked, but their names were not there," she said.
Esther said the scheme had been flagged by the Health Ministry in 2023 but remained active, with reports of recent activity.
She also alleged the operator falsely claimed affiliation with Infection Prevention and Control (IPAC), while participants were exposed to invasive procedures such as Botox injections, polydioxanone (PDO) thread procedures and laser treatments despite not being medical practitioners.
Medical Aesthetic and Innovation Centre (USMARI) chairman Associate Professor Dr Ungku Mohd Shahrin said only non-invasive aesthetic procedures should be performed by non-medical personnel.
He said minimal invasive procedures involving injectables and laser treatments require extensive medical training and certification, taking seven to nine years under Health Ministry supervision.
"Patient safety is our utmost concern. Proper accreditation and competency are essential," he added.
Chong said investigations are ongoing and legal action will be taken.
He also urged those who may have enrolled in similar programmes have been asked to come forward and verify the legitimacy of the courses and qualifications with the relevant authorities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police bust illegal e-waste factories in Negri Sembilan, seize RM47mil in items
Article:
SEREMBAN: Police have busted illegal electronic waste (e-waste) processing operations and seized items worth more than RM47 million following raids on three factories over the past two months.
Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the raids, conducted under Ops Waste, were the result of continuous intelligence gathering.
He said one raid was carried out at an industrial site in Nilai on April 15, while two others were conducted in Kuala Sawah on May 6.
"During the Nilai raid, two local men, aged 43 and 37, believed to be caretakers of the premises, were detained. No arrests were made during the Kuala Sawah operations.
"The Department of Environment (DOE) seized suspected e-waste and 39 machines with an estimated value of more than RM47.637 million. They also detected three offences under the Environmental Quality Act 1974 (Amendment) 2024," he said at a press conference today.
Also present was Negri Sembilan DOE director Abdul Aziz Parmin.
Alzafny said the Seremban City Council (MBS) issued compounds under the Advertisement By-Laws and ordered all three premises to cease operations immediately for operating without valid licences.
He said the integrated operation involved the Internal Security and Public Order Department, the DOE, MBS, and the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry
"Police and relevant agencies will continue monitoring to curb illegal e-waste processing, which threatens public health and the environment," he said.
In a separate development, Alzafny said police would continue monitoring social media for provocative or inciting comments that could disrupt public order in relation to the ongoing crisis in the state.
He said police are finalising the investigation papers to be submitted to the deputy public prosecutor's office.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC denies claims of barring public from filing complaints
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has denied claims that it bars people from filing complaints, saying the allegations are false and aimed at tarnishing its image.
The MACC said it welcomes complaints from anyone, regardless of their position or background, but all visitors must comply with standard security procedures.
"Access control at MACC premises is a standard practice implemented consistently for all visitors and is neither discriminatory nor intended to prevent any party from lodging a report," the commission said in a statement today.
It said its premises fall under the Protected Areas and Protected Places Act 1959, meaning only authorised individuals may enter and all visitors are subject to security checks.
The MACC also denied allegations of gender-related issues in its complaint and statement-taking process, saying officers are bound by professional and legal standards at all times.
The commission said it would not allow its security procedures to be used to paint it as obstructing the public from making reports. It reaffirmed its commitment to carrying out its work professionally, independently, and with integrity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan 'robot wolves' in high demand to scare off bears
Article:
TOKYO: A Japanese company making ferocious-looking robot wolves is being swamped by orders after record numbers of fatal bear attacks on humans last year.
"Monster Wolf" is an animatoronic scarecrow with flashing red eyes that howls and growls menacingly to scare away wild animals.
Ohta Seiki, the Hokkaido-based firm that makes the devices, has already received around 50 orders this year, more than the usual volume for an entire year.
"We make them by hand. We cannot make them fast enough now. We are asking our customers to wait two to three months," company president Yuji Ohta told AFP.
"Awareness of bear safety and measures against wildlife damage (on farm products) improved. There was also a growing recognition that our product is effective in dealing with bears," Ohta said.
Orders come mostly from farmers, operators of golf courses and people working outside in rural areas such as in construction.
Bears killed 13 people across Japan in 2025-2026, more than twice the previous high.
More than 50,000 bear sightings were recorded nationwide, more than double the previous record set two years before, according to official data.
The animals were seen entering homes, roaming near schools and rampaging through supermarkets and hot spring resorts on an almost daily basis.
The number of bears captured and then culled nearly tripled from a year earlier to 14,601, also marking an all-time high.
Some northern regions also reported in April more than four times as many sightings as last year as the animals emerge from hibernation, local media said.
"Monster Wolf" features artificial fur draped over an assembled pipe frame, attached to speakers, topped with a menacing, open-mouthed face.
For prices starting from around US$4,000 or higher, the system comes with a battery, solar panels, sensors, speakers and other apparatus.
It broadcasts more than 50 kinds of recorded sounds, including human voices and electronic noises, audible up to one kilometre away.
The device turns its head from side to side, flashes red LED eyes, while its tail is equipped with blue LEDs.
Ohta introduced the product in 2016 to prevent damage to agricultural products by deer, boars and bears, and it was initially derided as a gimmick.
The company is now upgrading the device by putting it on wheels to chase animals or patrol specific paths.
Ohta also plans to develop a hand-held version for hikers, anglers and schoolchildren, while also exploring artificial intelligence cameras for future models.
"We wanted to apply our manufacturing to do our part to deal with bears," he said. AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kepong restaurant assault victim fears for safety after unprovoked attack
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The victim of a brutal assault at a Bak Kut Teh restaurant in Jalan Besar Kepong last week is now fearing for his life, and that of his family, following reports that his assailants could be linked to secret societies.
He is afraid that the assailants, who are currently in police custody will come looking for him if they are released on bail.
The man, who requested anonymity due to safety concerns, said he has been left traumatised and continues to suffer physically and emotionally from the incident which he described as totally unprovoked.
The victim, an entrepreneur and company director, said he now struggles with sleepless nights and no longer dares to dine out alone.
He sought the help of his elected rep, Kepong MP Lim Lip Eng, to highlight what happened early Thursday morning. His account was later posted on Lim's Facebook.
"Nothing happened before the attack started. I woke up early, was hungry and went there (restaurant) to have my favourite bak kut teh. I usually patronise both branches either with family, friends or by myself.
"While eating (alone), I heard some noise to my right, looked up and saw some guys drinking, then turned away to continue eating.
"Then I heard somebody shout in Cantonese, 'What the **** are you looking at? but I continued eating as I did not know these individuals.
"Ice cubes were then thrown at me and the next thing I knew I was viciously attacked and punched for no apparent reason."
The man said he did not provoke, scold or challenge his assailants as he was already in a daze after the first punches landed on him. He said he tried to defend himself only once and that did not help.
From closed-circuit television camera footage, no one was seen going to the victim's aid while he was being attacked. A handful of customers were seen hurrying away when the attack started.
"If I had challenged or fought back, I believe they would have killed me. I was also lucky there were no knives lying around or I would have been mercilessly stabbed to death."
Apart from being punched, kicked and slapped, the victim was also assaulted with chairs and even a kettle.
He sustained serious injuries to his head, eyes, neck, shoulders, back and body and stitches to his forehead.
He said he is still in pain, having frequent headaches and his eyesight was also sometimes blurry, which was not the case before the incident.
"I am now seeing numerous medical doctors. A neurosurgeon, orthopaedic surgeon and ophthalmology specialist who were referred by the hospital's emergency department. I am also seeing a psychiatrist for the trauma," the man said.
He expressed relief that the suspects have been arrested and remanded and hoped they will not get away scot free only to harm others.
In a statement, the restaurant said the incident took place in the early hours of Thursday, close to closing time.
The eatery said its staff immediately informed the person in charge and lodged a police report.
According to staff members who were present, the incident unfolded suddenly, causing panic and confusion among employees and diners.
The restaurant also expressed concern for the victim, saying its representatives met the man the following day and are monitoring his condition.
"We are cooperating with police and have provided them with CCTV footage to assist with the investigation," the restaurant said.
Police have since arrested three men between the ages of 30 and 50 to assist in the investigations.
Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni had said the investigation paper had been referred to the deputy public prosecutor for further instructions.
Police are also investigating possible links between the suspects and secret societies.
The case is being investigated under Section 324 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt using weapons or dangerous means, and Section 427 for committing mischief and causing damage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New MACC chief vows tougher, independent anti-graft drive rooted in rule of law
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman has pledged to steer the anti-graft agency with a firmer and more independent approach grounded in the rule of law, while vowing there would be no compromise against corruption and abuse of power.
In his first official statement after assuming office today, Abd Halim said the commission would act without fear or favour against any individual who misused their position or influence.
"With my experience in the judiciary, I will bring with me a strong foundation based on the rule of law and a determination not to compromise the process of justice under any circumstances.
"I hope to lead the formation of an MACC that is firmer, more credible and fully independent, while ensuring the commission can act without fear or favour," he said.
Abd Halim said that corruption in any form is unacceptable and described it as "the greatest betrayal of the people" as well as a serious threat to the nation's future.
He said the MACC under his leadership would enforce the law with greater firmness, fairness and professionalism, regardless of rank, position or influence.
"I will ensure the MACC acts with greater firmness, fair justice and without compromise against any party abusing power, regardless of rank, position or influence," he said.
The 69-year-old former High Court judge also pledged to restore public confidence in the anti-graft agency by strengthening enforcement, improving transparency and ensuring investigations were conducted with the highest level of professionalism and comprehensive justice.
He said preventive measures would continue to be enhanced, including stronger protection for whistleblowers and expanded strategic cooperation with stakeholders to cultivate a culture of integrity nationwide.
"Restoring public confidence is not an option, but a demand that cannot be delayed to ensure the MACC remains strong, transparent and accountable, fully guided by the rule of law," he said.
Abd Halim also called on Malaysians to unite in rejecting corruption, describing the fight against graft as a shared national responsibility.
"With determination and unity, we can build a nation founded on integrity, justice and trust," he said.
Abd Halim officially reported for duty as the seventh MACC chief commissioner today, replacing Tan Sri Azam Baki, who completed his tenure yesterday after six years at the helm of the commission.
Born in Kampung Alai, Melaka, on March 10, 1957, Abd Halim holds a Bachelor of Laws degree from the University of Malaya and a Master of Laws degree from the University of Hull in the United Kingdom.
He began his legal career in 1982 as a federal counsel at the Attorney-General's Chambers before serving as a magistrate in Kluang and Muar, as well as senior assistant registrar at the Malacca High Court.
Abd Halim later served as a Sessions Court judge in several locations, including Tawau, Sandakan, Kuala Lumpur, George Town and Ampang, before being appointed head of the Research Division at the Office of the Chief Justice in 2003.
He was appointed judicial commissioner in 2005 before serving as a High Court judge in Penang, Johor Baru and Shah Alam between 2009 and 2023.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Besieged Starmer seeks to heal Labour divisions in King's Speech
Article:
LONDON: King Charles III will on Wednesday outline UK leader Keir Starmer's legislative plans, during a pomp-filled ceremony that could have huge repercussions for the embattled prime minister's future.
Starmer, fighting to face down a revolt within his ruling Labour Party, has promised his government will be "better" and bolder to assuage disgruntled voters impatient for change.
That pledge will be put to the test in Wednesday's address in parliament which Downing Street said will unveil an "ambitious programme" to make Britain a "stronger, fairer" country.
It is the latest crunch moment in Starmer's 22-month premiership and comes after Labour descended into open warfare over the prime minister's fate following the party's heavy defeats in local and regional elections.
Four junior ministers resigned and the number of MPs urging Starmer to quit passed 80, but more than 100 others signed a statement urging colleagues to back him.
On Wednesday, health minister Wes Streeting met Starmer at  Downing Street, as speculation swirled over whether he could launch a leadership bid.
The meeting, billed as a "showdown" by British media, lasted less than 20 minutes and he left without commenting.
Streeting is popular on the right of Labour, while a would-be challenger on the left could be former deputy prime minister Angela Rayner.
Another much-touted contender, Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester in northwest England, is unable to stand currently as he is not an MP.
His supporters want Starmer to lay out a timetable for his departure that allows Burnham to return to parliament and stand.
Under party rules, any challenger would need the support of 81 Labour MPs – 20 percent of the party in parliament – to trigger a leadership contest.
Starmer has vowed to fight any challenge.
Trade unions that support Labour and have a say in decision-making on Wednesday rejected Starmer, issuing a statement saying "it's clear that the prime minister will not lead Labour into the next election" and a plan must be made to elect a new leader.
Several senior ministers rallied around the Labour premier: Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy urged lawmakers to "step back and take a breath", while a spokesman for interior minister Shabana Mahmood denied rumours that she was resigning.
"Britain stands at a pivotal moment," Starmer, who in July 2024 became the UK's sixth prime minister in eight years, said late Tuesday ahead of the King's Speech.
"To press ahead with a plan to build a stronger, fairer country or turn back to the chaos and instability of the past."
Despite its name, the King's Speech is not written by the monarch but by the government, which uses it to detail the laws it proposes over the next 12 months.
Downing Street said the address will include more than 35 bills "to bolster economic, energy, (and) national security.
They will include proposals to fully nationalise British Steel and deepen Britain's relationship with the European Union.
King Charles will deliver the proposals from a golden throne in the House of Lords upper chamber while wearing the diamond-studded Imperial State Crown and a long crimson robe.
The day's proceedings start when royal bodyguards ritually search the basement of the Palace of Westminster for explosives – a legacy of the failed attempt by Catholics to blow up parliament in the 1605 Gunpowder plot.
The sovereign will then travel to the Houses of Parliament by carriage from Buckingham Palace, escorted by mounted cavalry.
Tradition dictates that an MP is ceremonially held "hostage" in the palace to ensure the king's safe return.
A parliamentary official known as Black Rod will have the door of the lower chamber House of Commons slammed in their face, a tradition that symbolises parliament's independence from the monarchy.
MPs will follow Black Rod to the upper chamber, where Charles will give the speech to assembled lords and ladies in red and ermine robes, plus invited members of the elected Commons at around 11.30 am (1030 GMT).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 21-year-old university student among three injured in express bus crash
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 21-year-old private university student was among three people injured after an express bus crashed into a road milling machine at Km102.4 of the North-South Expressway (northbound) near Yong Peng early today.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shahrulanuar Mushaddat Abdullah Sani said the student suffered injuries to her mouth.
Apart from the student, also injured during the accident were the 42-year-old bus driver and another bus passenger, 56, who suffered head injuries.
Shahrulanuar said the express bus was travelling from Bandar Penawar, Kota Tinggi, to Kuala Lumpur carrying 26 passengers.
He said preliminary investigations showed that the highway concessionaire was carrying out road resurfacing works between Km101 and Km103, with the slow lane and emergency lane closed in a diagonal formation.
"The bus driver is believed to have lost control of the vehicle before it crashed into the road milling machine at the work site," Shahrulanuar said.
The driver suffered chest injuries. All three victims were taken to Sultanah Fatimah Specialist Hospital in Muar for treatment.
Shahrulanuar said the remaining passengers, comprising 14 men and 12 women, escaped unhurt.
The case is being investigated under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for careless and inconsiderate driving.
Earlier today, it was reported that
the bus
had slammed into the road paving machine at the scene.
A team from the Yong Peng fire and rescue station was deployed and arrived at the scene about 1.05am following an emergency call.
Upon arrival, the fire and rescue team found the bus driver pinned in his seat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas delivers Malaysia's first sustainable aviation fuel to KLIA
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) has delivered Malaysia's first blended sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) to Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA), marking a key milestone in the country's push towards lower-carbon aviation.
Locally blended at Petronas' blending facilities, the International Sustainability & Carbon Certification-certified SAF was delivered to KLIA via Malaysian Refining Company's multi-product pipeline.
The fuel delivery was made for Malaysia Aviation Group (MAG).
"Decarbonising the aviation industry requires reliable pathways, and SAF represents one of the most immediate solutions available today.
"This pilot project is a strategic long-term investment to build in-house capabilities through advancements in technology and infrastructure, as well as talent development to deliver reliable, cost-effective solutions to our partners and customers," said Petronas vice president of refining, marketing and trading Ahmad Adly Alias.
The SAF will be uplifted for Malaysia Airlines' daily late evening MH2 service from Kuala Lumpur to London from Sept 1-16, marking a tangible step in integrating SAF into its regular operations.
MAG group chief sustainability Philip See every flight powered by SAF proves that aviation decarbonisation is not just a future ambition, but an achievable reality today, demonstrating that practical and scalable actions can already be taken across operations to significantly reduce emissions.
See said strong collaboration across the aviation industry is essential to scale up SAF production, making it more widely available, accessible, and affordable for the industry.
"MAG is encouraged by this progress and remains committed to champion initiatives that accelerate SAF adoption, supporting both Malaysia's and the global journey towards a greener, more sustainable future for aviation," he added.
The achievement underscores Petronas' readiness to pave the way for scalable, cost-efficient SAF supply within Malaysia, backed by end-to-end capability from global procurement and certification to local blending and distribution, all in compliance with rigorous international sustainability standards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi, Nik Nazmi to announce new political direction this Sunday
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pandan member of Parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad will announce their new political direction on Sunday.
Rafizi, who is the former economy minister, said the past year had given both of them the space to weigh various views, considerations and factors before deciding on their next political path.
"The past year has given us both the opportunity to take into account various views, considerations and factors in deciding our next political direction," he said in a statement today.
The announcement will be made at a public event at the PJ Performing Arts Centre at One Utama E, Bandar Utama, Petaling Jaya, from 2pm to 5pm.
"I invite Malaysians from across the country and from various backgrounds who wish to foster a progressive political stream to attend this event," Rafizi said.
On May 28 last year, Nik Nazmi, who was the natural resources and environmental sustainability minister, said he had submitted his resignation letter to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, effective July 4.
His announcement came about three hours after Rafizi said he was resigning as minister, effective June 17.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Commission steps up engagement ahead of consumer credit act implementation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Consumer Credit Commission (CCC) is stepping up engagement with industry players ahead of the implementation of the Consumer Credit Act 2025 (CCA), which will bring non-bank credit and credit service providers under a formal regulatory framework.
The commission said the authorisation framework, covering licensing and registration, marks a significant milestone in strengthening oversight of the consumer credit industry.
As part of preparations, CCC will organise industry briefing sessions on May 18 and 19 to help affected businesses understand the new regulatory requirements and prepare for compliance before the rollout in June.
Under Phase 1 of the CCA implementation, six business activities will fall within the commission's regulatory scope.
They comprise buy now pay later (BNPL), factoring, leasing, debt collection, impaired loan or financing acquisition as well as debt counselling and management.
CCC said it has reached out to entities identified as operating within these sectors to participate in the engagement sessions.
Companies that have not yet been identified but are involved in the regulated activities have also been urged to register their interest through CCC's official website to be included in the regulatory engagement process.
CCC executive chairman Abu Hassan Alshari Yahaya said constructive engagement with the industry is essential to ensure businesses are fully aware of their obligations under the new law.
"Early engagement will support a smooth transition and contribute to a more transparent and responsible consumer credit industry," he added.
According to the commission, the CCA and the establishment of the commission form part of the government's broader reform agenda to create a more transparent, responsible and inclusive consumer credit ecosystem.
Licensing and registration requirements under the act will take effect from June 1 this year.
Industry players will be given a six-month transition period to apply for the relevant licence or registration, with CCC warning that enforcement action may be taken against entities that fail to comply.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cleaner pleads guilty to sexually assaulting two teens at rehab centre
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: A cleaner pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court today to two counts of physical sexual assault against two teenage boys at a Social Welfare Department rehabilitation centre.
Noor Azlan Mat Nayan, 57, entered the plea after the charges were read to him before Judge Zulhazmi Abdullah.
According to the first charge, he committed physical sexual assault against a 14-year-old boy in a toilet next to a vocational workshop at the Seberang Prai Utara institution at 11am on April 21.
For the second charge, Noor Azlan was alleged to have committed the same act against a 16-year-old boy at the same location at 9am in early April.
Both charges were framed under Section 14(b) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017.
The offence carries a maximum prison sentence of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Wan Amira Ruzaini Wan Abdul Razak requested the court to fix a date for the facts and sentencing.
She also urged the court not to offer bail, citing the gravity of the offences involving sexual crimes against minors.
The accused did not apply for bail, stating that his family members lived far away.
Zulhazmi then fixed June 24 for the facts and sentencing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang seeks RM500 million federal funding for Gebeng halal hub development
Article:
KUANTAN: Pahang will submit an application to secure an allocation of RM500 million to the federal government for the construction of the Gebeng Halal Hub here.
State Investment, Industry, Science, Technology and Innovation Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Nizar Mohamad Najib said the funds are intended for infrastructure development in an area covering 295 hectares.
"The area is currently undeveloped. The Gebeng Halal Hub will be implemented in phases over a duration of 15 years.
"Pahang welcomes the involvement of private parties and investors interested in participating in the development of the site by providing industrial lots, constructing factories, and building logistics facilities such as warehouses.
"A company from China has recently expressed interest in collaborating on the development and bringing investment into this industrial park," he told the Pahang state assembly during the question-and-answer session at Wisma Sri Pahang today.
He said the targeted industries for the Gebeng Halal Hub include halal and non-halal food manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, agricultural-and meat-based products, as well as halal logistics and related services.
Nizar was replying to a question from Datuk Andansura Rabu (Pas–Beserah) on the latest status of the Gebeng Halal Hub development.
Meanwhile, Pahang Unity, Tourism and Culture Committee chairman Leong Yu Man said RM15 million has been approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan to implement tourism infrastructure development in Cherating to strengthen the tourism sector.
She said the project, implemented by the Kuantan City Council, is currently at the design and planning stage and is expected to be completed by September this year.
"It includes parking facilities, a tourism event plaza (open space), and an esplanade," she said.
Leong was replying to a supplementary question from Datuk Ahmad Irshadi Abdullah Al-Qari on measures to boost tourism in Cherating, following remarks by Tengku Mahkota Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah last week.
The Tengku Mahkota questioned why Cherating, once a world-renowned destination, now appears to have been
neglected
in his speech at the opening of the 15th State Legislative Assembly sitting.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Najib obtains stay of execution on US$1.18bil SRC judgment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Najib Razak today obtained a stay of execution on a High Court judgment ordering him to pay US$1.18 billion pending his appeal over losses suffered by SRC International Sdn Bhd.
Judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin ruled that special circumstances justified the stay, citing the unprecedented magnitude of the judgment sum.
"The judgment sum in the present case is in excess of RM5 billion.
"The court is not aware of any precedent where a monetary judgment of this magnitude has been entered against a single individual," he said.
Ahmad Fairuz said enforcing the judgment immediately could cause irreparable prejudice to the former prime minister, while SRC's entitlement to the judgment sum would only be deferred pending the appeal.
He said the appellate court had previously granted stays in a separate Inland Revenue Board lawsuit involving Najib, and the court should adopt a consistent judicial approach.
"The balance of justice leans in favour of the grant of stay. The plaintiff's entitlement to the fruits of the judgment is not extinguished. It is deferred pending determination of the appeal," he said.
The court also ordered Najib to pay RM2 million in costs to SRC for the main suit, subject to allocation.
In third-party proceedings, Najib was ordered to pay RM150,000 in costs to each set of solicitors involved.
The third-party defendants are former SRC directors Datuk Suboh Md Yassin, Datuk Mohamed Azhar Osman Khairuddin, Datuk Shahrol Azral Ibrahim Halmi, Tan Sri Ismee Ismail, and Datuk Che Abdullah@ Rashidi Che Omar.
Najib had sought indemnity or contribution from them should he be held liable in SRC's US$1.18 billion civil claim, but the court dismissed his application.
On March 31, the court found Najib liable for losses suffered by the company after ruling that he had committed misfeasance in public office.
The court held that Najib had directed the board through shareholder resolutions and executed control through the appointment of directors personally loyal to him, as well as through his authority as prime minister and finance minister.
It added that the SRC board never independently evaluated strategic directions proposed by the former Pekan member of parliament, as Najib exercised complete control over the company's financial decisions.
The court further noted that Najib's actions were driven by personal gain with the specific intent to injure SRC for personal financial enrichment.
On July 28, 2020, Najib was sentenced to 12 years' imprisonment and fined RM210 million after being found guilty of abuse of power, criminal breach of trust, and money laundering involving RM42 million of SRC funds.
However, the Federal Territories Pardons Board later halved his jail term to six years and reduced the fine to RM50 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia hits 10.64 million international arrivals in Q1 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia recorded a new tourism milestone in the first quarter of this year as international arrivals rose 5.4 per cent to 10.64 million visitors, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said.
Tiong said the latest figures marked the second consecutive year the country exceeded 10 million international arrivals in the first quarter, surpassing even pre-pandemic levels.
He said this year's growth was driven largely by the Chinese New Year holidays, which helped Malaysia set a new monthly record in February.
"For the first time, visitor arrivals in February surpassed three million, reaching 3,472,557 arrivals, the highest ever recorded in a single month," he said in a statement today.
He said the latest figure was also higher than the pre-pandemic level in 2019, when Malaysia recorded 9.01 million visitors in the first quarter.
Tiong said the achievement was recorded despite geopolitical tensions in the Middle East affecting fuel supply, forcing flight route changes and pushing up airline operating costs, with the impact becoming more apparent towards the end of March.
"Because of this, the first-quarter visitor arrival performance can still be considered good. By region, only visitors from three regions recorded a decline, while six others posted growth compared with the same period last year," he said.
He said arrivals from the Middle East were the worst affected, falling 27.2 per cent, while South Asia and North Africa also declined by 6.7 per cent and 8.4 per cent, respectively.
"Visitors from East Asia and asean each rose by more than 200,000, while arrivals from the Americas, Oceania and Europe also increased by more than 10,000.
"Central Asia recorded the highest growth rate, rising 20 per cent compared with last year," he said.
Tiong said China was the main growth driver, with more than 280,000 additional visitors and a 25.2 per cent increase, while Australia recorded double-digit growth of 11.4 per cent.
Tiong said the expansion of Malaysia's international air connectivity, especially through direct routes, remained a key driver of tourism growth and contributed to the 5.4 per cent increase in first-quarter arrivals.
He said 20 new scheduled routes began operating in the first three months of the year, with 12 airlines adding 95 international flights to Malaysia each week, while five other airlines operated six charter routes linking China and Hong Kong with Malaysia.
He said increased China-Malaysia flight capacity during the Chinese New Year travel period helped arrivals from China surpass 500,000, reaching 604,675 and contributing to February's record monthly arrivals.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Italy tests two people for hantavirus amid quarantine measures
Article:
ROME: Italy is testing biological samples from two people for possible hantavirus infection, an Argentine tourist hospitalised with pneumonia and a man from Calabria in voluntary isolation, the Health Ministry said on Tuesday.
The Argentine tourist had left an endemic area back home on April 30 and traveled to Italy on a Buenos Aires-Rome flight before later going to Sicily, where she was hospitalised with pneumonia, the ministry said in a statement.
Local health authorities requested the test on Tuesday, and her sample was sent to the Spallanzani infectious diseases hospital in Rome, where it will be analysed alongside a sample from the 25-year-old Calabrian man, who had briefly come into contact with a Dutch woman who later died from the virus.
BRITISH TOURIST IN QUARANTINE
In a separate development, a British tourist was located in Milan and placed in quarantine after British authorities warned he had been on the same flight as the Dutch woman, the Health Ministry said.
A companion traveling with him was also taken to hospital as a precaution. Hantavirus is primarily spread by rodents but can be transmitted between people in rare cases, according to the World Health Organization. It usually begins with flu-like symptoms, such as fatigue and fever, one to eight weeks after exposure.
A cluster has been linked in recent days to the MV Hondius ship, which docked in Spain's Canary Islands following a polar expedition that departed from Argentina. The WHO has increased its tally of confirmed cases in the outbreak to nine. It said further cases could materialise because of the long incubation period, but that this was not a pandemic, and nothing like COVID-19.
The British tourist had traveled on a St. Helena–Johannesburg flight on which Mirjam Schilperoord, the wife of the first victim, was also traveling.
Schilperoord transferred to a KLM flight bound for the Netherlands but the crew decided she was too sick to fly and removed her. She collapsed at the airport and died the next day.
The Calabrian man had also been on the KLM flight and even though any exposure to Schilperoord would have been brief, medical authorities are keeping passengers and crew in several countries under close observation. --REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops cut power to businesses fronting illegal gambling dens
Article:
KLUANG: Police cut electricity supply to six premises suspected of operating illegal public lottery and online gambling activities during an integrated operation here yesterday.
The joint operation, conducted at 9am, involved police and Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) personnel.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Bahrin Mohd Noh said the premises were identified as operating illegal public lottery outlets and online gambling activities through top-up systems within the district.
He said electricity supply to the premises was cut under Section 21A(1) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953.
"The operation was carried out as part of continuous enforcement efforts to curb illegal gambling activities and other unlawful operations in Kluang," he said in a statement today.
Bahrin said similar integrated operations would continue to ensure premises involved in illegal gambling activities were brought to book.
He also urged members of the public with information on illegal gambling activities to contact the nearest police station or the district police headquarters hotline at 07-7766822 or 07-7784222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's MRO sector on track to RM55bil target
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) industry is on track to achieve its RM55 billion revenue target by end-2030 driven by rising aircraft demand across Asia Pacific, increasing aerospace investments and growing local engineering capabilities.
Deputy Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Sim Tze Tzin said the revenue target represents about 20 per cent of the projected growth in the Asia Pacific MRO market, which is forecasted to exceed US$60 billion by 2030.
"Our MRO segment is the true heart of the industry, providing high-value livelihoods for over 12,000 workers."
"We cannot be complacent as the industry evolves and regional competition intensify. Malaysia must move further up the value chain.
"We are shifting our focus towards higher value MRO segments such as components and engineering competence," he said during the opening of the MRO Southeast Asia 2026 conference here today.
Sim said Malaysia's total aerospace sector generated RM32.5 billion in revenue in 2025 supported by over 250 companies and a workforce of 34,700 skilled Malaysians.
He said the aerospace industry has evolved into a major economic growth sector under the National Industrial Master Plan 2030 and the Malaysia Aerospace Industry Blueprint 2030.
"In the early 2000s, Malaysia's aerospace industry was still developing. Today, Malaysia through Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) has become a regional hub for MRO," he said.
Sim added that major global aerospace companies such as Airbus, Boeing, GE Aerospace, Safran and Turkish Aerospace Industries have expanded their presence in Malaysia, reflecting confidence in the country's engineering and technical talent.
He said the government is now shifting its focus towards higher-value aerospace activities, including component MRO, engineering competencies and aerospace parts manufacturing as regional competition intensifies.
"We want to create more ecosystem for aerospace, be it in design manufacturing, R&D and also MRO," he said.
Sim also said the government is strengthening infrastructure to support the sector's expansion, including regeneration of Sultan Abdul Aziz Shah Airport or Subang Airport and the continued development of the Selangor Aero Park within Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Aeropolis.
The two-day MRO Southeast Asia 2026 conference gathered over 350 participants from airlines, original equipment manufacturers, MRO providers, investors and policymakers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank of America relocates to Merdeka 118
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank of America has officially inaugurated its new office on the 38th floor of Merdeka 118, marking another milestone in its long-standing presence in Malaysia since first establishing operations in the country in 1959.
The move to Merdeka 118, one of the world's tallest buildings and a new landmark in Kuala Lumpur, reflects the bank's continued commitment to Malaysia and its strategy to grow alongside the country's increasing importance in regional and global capital markets.
Now in its 67th year in Malaysia, Bank of America employs more than 90 staff locally and offers a full suite of financial services spanning corporate banking, investment banking, global markets and treasury solutions.
The office opening comes amid the bank's expanding involvement in Malaysia's broader economic and investment initiatives.
Recently, Bank of America signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the Malaysian government to promote the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) to its network of North American investors and multinational corporations, highlighting rising international interest in the flagship cross-border development corridor.
The collaboration is expected to strengthen Malaysia's investment promotion efforts by leveraging Bank of America's extensive global corporate and institutional network to attract high-value foreign direct investments into strategic sectors within the JS-SEZ.
The initiative also underscores growing confidence among major international financial institutions in the long-term prospects of the Johor-Singapore corridor, which is being positioned as a regional hub for advanced manufacturing, digital economy activities, logistics, renewable energy and high-value services.
The JS-SEZ has increasingly emerged as a key investment destination due to its strategic location, integrated supply chain ecosystem and close connectivity with Singapore, one of Asia's leading financial and commercial centres. The economic zone is aimed at enhancing cross-border integration between Johor and Singapore while facilitating smoother trade, investment and talent mobility.
Industry observers noted that the involvement of a major Wall Street institution such as Bank of America could further raise the global profile of the JS-SEZ and reinforce investor confidence in Malaysia's economic transformation agenda and regional competitiveness.
According to Bernama, Bank of America said it remains committed to delivering "profits with purpose" as it continues to drive responsible growth for its employees, clients and communities in Malaysia.
The new office at Merdeka 118 was inaugurated by Bernard Mensah alongside Jin Su and Gautam Puntambekar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam denies dealings with Albert Tei, says ready to face probe
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has denied allegations made against him by businessman Albert Tei.
Responding to Tei's latest claims, Azam said Tei was free to lodge any formal complaint with the relevant enforcement agencies and said that he is prepared to cooperate with any investigation.
"In relation to Albert Tei's latest allegations against me, I would like to make a brief statement.
"I have never met, dealt with or directly recorded any statement from him.
"He is free to lodge any official complaint with any enforcement agency regarding those allegations and I am ready to be investigated by any agency in relation to the claims," he told the New Straits Times.
Tei had previously alleged that Azam had threatened, obstructed and victimised him when he attempted to expose an alleged corruption scandal involving 15 high-profile Sabah politicians in November 2024.
Tei claimed to possess evidence, including WhatsApp communication records, witness lists and related documents, to support his allegations against Azam.
He also claimed that he had refrained from lodging a police report or speaking publicly earlier because Azam was still serving as MACC chief commissioner at the time.
Tei added that he had since lodged a police report at the Putrajaya district police headquarters and intended to submit all related documents to the new MACC leadership for further investigation.
Earlier today, Tei claimed he was prevented from entering the MACC headquarters here when he attempted to lodge a report related to Azam.
The incident sparked several minutes of commotion at the entrance of the building following a dispute over the number of lawyers allowed to accompany him while submitting the report.
Tei alleged that MACC personnel allowed only one lawyer to accompany him, despite his request to be accompanied by two lawyers, including a female lawyer.
"I came to lodge an official report, but there was an obstruction at the entrance when only one lawyer was allowed to enter with me.
"I wanted both my lawyers present because one of them is a female lawyer who is involved in documentation matters and observing the process," he claimed during a press conference outside the MACC headquarters.
Tei questioned the commission's transparency and enforcement procedures, claiming he attended as a complainant and not as a suspect.
He also claimed that the request to bring a female lawyer was made for safety and comfort reasons in the event the investigating officer handling the report was a woman.
Tei alleged that officers refused to show any written instruction regarding the restriction and informed him only that it was an "order from higher-ups".
He also alleged that the officer who prevented him from entering had links to Azam, although no evidence was produced to support the claim.
Following the dispute, Tei and his legal team initially left the MACC headquarters without lodging the report.
However, media practitioners later understood that MACC had subsequently allowed Tei to enter and submit the report, but he declined to proceed and instead demanded that the commission issue an official statement over the confusion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump vows to push Xi to 'open up' China at superpower summit [WATCH]
Article:
BEIJING: US President Donald Trump said he would ask Xi Jinping to "open up" China to American firms as he headed to Beijing on Wednesday for a high-stakes summit that will also bring up the Iran war.
In a sign of Trump's focus on business, Nvidia chief Jensen Huang boarded Air Force One during a stopover in Alaska, with Tesla's Elon Musk also travelling on the presidential plane to China.
"I will be asking President Xi, a Leader of extraordinary distinction, to 'open up' China so that these brilliant people can work their magic, and help bring the People's Republic to an even higher level!" Trump wrote on social media after departing Washington.
A host of other top CEOs, including Apple's Tim Cook, will also be in Beijing for the visit, the first by a US president to China in nearly a decade.
But Trump's ambitions to ramp up trade will have to contend with political frictions over Taiwan and the war in the Middle East, which already delayed the trip from March.
As he departed the White House, Trump said he expected a "long talk" with Xi about Iran, which sells most of its US-sanctioned oil to China.
But he also downplayed disagreements, telling reporters that "I don't think we need any help with Iran" from China and that Xi had been "relatively good" on the topic.
The Chinese foreign ministry said Wednesday it "welcomes" Trump's visit and that "China stands ready to work with the United States ... to expand cooperation and manage differences."
Yet Beijing is growing impatient for peace, with Foreign Minister Wang Yi urging his Pakistani counterpart on Tuesday to step up mediation efforts between Iran and the United States.
- 'BIG DEAL' -
This week's trip – the first since Trump visited Beijing in 2017 – will involve highly anticipated talks with Xi on Thursday and Friday, as well as lavish pomp and ceremony.
The packed itinerary includes a state banquet in Beijing's Great Hall of the People and a tea reception.
Trump said Monday he would speak to Xi about US arms sales to Taiwan, the self-governing democracy claimed by China – a departure from historic US insistence that it will not consult Beijing on its support to the island.
China's controls on rare earth exports, AI rivalry and the countries' raucous trade relationship are also among the topics expected to be taken up by the heads of the world's top two economies.
The two sides are set to discuss extending a one-year truce in their tariff war, which Trump and Xi reached during their last meeting in South Korea in October.
The tense buildup to the superpower summit was already visible on the streets of Beijing, with police monitoring major intersections and checking the ID cards of passengers on the metro, AFP journalists saw.
"It's definitely a big deal," said Wen Wen, a 24-year-old woman travelling from the eastern city of Nanjing, when asked by AFP about Trump's visit.
"Some progress will certainly be made," she said, noting that she hopes China and the United States can ensure "lasting peace" despite "recent instability in the global situation."
- 'VERY GOOD RELATIONSHIP' -
The United States and China have long sought to stabilise their relationship despite increasingly seeing each other as adversaries in trade and geopolitics.
Trump has repeatedly touted a strong personal relationship with Xi, which he insisted on Monday would prevent a Chinese invasion of Taiwan, the self-ruled island claimed by Beijing.
"I think we'll be fine. I have a very good relationship with President Xi. He knows I don't want that to happen," he said.
Trump's trip will be closely scrutinised by Taiwan and Asian allies for any sign of weakening US support.
Beijing has grown more confident and assertive since Trump's 2017 trip and the US president finds himself in a weakened position as he seeks a way out of his Iran war.
But the summit also comes at an uncertain time for China's economy, which has struggled in recent years with sluggish domestic spending and a protracted debt crisis in the once-booming property sector.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM directs ministries to engage hawkers over low aid participation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed relevant ministries to engage hawkers following findings that participation in formal financial assistance programmes among the group remains very low.
Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said a study by the Khazanah Research Institute found that only five per cent of hawkers received assistance through programmes such as Bank Simpanan Nasional, Tekun Nasional and Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia.
"During today's cabinet meeting, the prime minister raised the findings of a study which showed that participation among hawkers in formal financial assistance programmes remains very low.
"Among the issues identified was the business status of hawkers, many of whom are not formally registered.
"Based on the study, only five per cent of hawkers received assistance.
"As such, the prime minister has asked relevant ministries to conduct engagement sessions with hawkers to better understand the issues faced and ensure formal financial assistance can be extended to them,"he said during a post-cabinet press conference today.
Fahmi, who is also communications minister, said the cabinet had also been briefed on the launch of the Madani Mediation Centre, a pro bono initiative aimed at providing free mediation services for selected disputes.
He said the initiative is managed by the Legal Affairs Division under the Prime Minister's Department.
He added that the centre would focus on commercial mediation involving disputes below RM250,000.
"At present, there are more than 170 qualified mediators available for cases requiring mediation under the specified amount, and the services will be provided free of charge."
The centre is scheduled to be officially launched in Kuala Lumpur on May 18 by Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Fahmi: Jho Low pardon to be raised at cabinet meeting next week
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The cabinet is expected to discuss next week reports that fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, or Jho Low, has sought a pardon from United States President Donald Trump.
Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was not discussed during today's cabinet meeting.
"The issue arose while the cabinet meeting was ongoing and was not discussed today.
"However, it will certainly be brought to the cabinet's attention next week," he said during a post-cabinet press conference today.
Fahmi was responding to a question on whether the Malaysian government planned to make formal representations to the United States authorities opposing any possible pardon for Jho Low.
Earlier, it was reported that Low, who is now a fugitive, had sought a
pardon from Trump,
according to a Wall Street Journal report published on Tuesday.
The request was filed in recent weeks, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing sources familiar with the matter, and if granted, it would remove criminal charges against him in the United States.
1MDB Asset Recovery Task Force chairman Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said
Low should not
be pardoned.
The Investment, Trade and Industry Minister said Low's request should be rejected and that the United States should instead assist Malaysia in locating him for further investigations.
"As far as I am concerned, I am against the pardon," he said.
Low faces multiple charges, including corruption and money laundering in the United States and Malaysia, over his alleged role in the misappropriation of at least US$4.5 billion from 1MDB.
He has consistently denied wrongdoing and his whereabouts remain unknown.
Low recently filed a request for a pardon that, if granted, would remove criminal charges against him in the United States.
A White House official reportedly said Low's request was not currently on the White House's radar.
The United States Department of Justice website lists a pending request for a "Pardon after Completion of Sentence" under Taek Jho Low, filed this year.
The Wall Street Journal also reported that Malaysia had temporarily lifted an Interpol Red Notice against Low, which would normally make him subject to arrest in most countries, to facilitate the return of significant assets to Malaysia.
In 2019, the United States struck a deal to recover about US$1 billion from Low, with the fugitive agreeing to surrender a private jet and high-end real estate in Beverly Hills, New York and London, among other assets.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said in 2023 that the government was negotiating with other countries to speed up Low's return, although he declined to name the countries involved.
Authorities previously said Low was believed to be in China, although Beijing has denied this.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd
